title: Loxococcus rupicola
taxonomy: Loxococcus rupicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Loxococcus is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae within the orchid family. The classification of Loxococcus rupicola places it within the tribe Coelogyninae. This classification is based on morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Loxococcus rupicola.

intro: Loxococcus rupicola, also known as the rock hyssop, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Loxococcus rupicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Loxococcus rupicola is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
